commit.message:
Remove JS text truncation in table formatters, let CSS line-clamp handle it

Removed the 60-char details truncation and 24-char secret truncation from
JS formatters — CSS 3-line clamp handles visual truncation, and the removed
JS truncation was preventing tooltip overflow detection from triggering.
Also improved tooltip handler with dual detection: JS truncation check
(tip length vs visible text) and CSS clamp check (unset clamp to measure
true scrollHeight).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix tooltip overflow detection for line-clamped cells

Previous scrollHeight check didn't work with -webkit-line-clamp.
Now temporarily unsets the clamp to measure true content height,
then compares against clamped height to decide if tooltip is needed.

commit.message:
Fix graph ordering: WEB01 after DC01, access→host edges

Pivot edges now prefer host→host (not access→host) so BFS layer
assignment places pivot destinations after the source host. Added
unconditional access→host edges so the chain flows correctly:
cred → access → host → [pivot] → next host.

commit.message:
Add via_access_id provenance tracking to kill-chain graph

Schema v3→v4 migration adds via_access_id FK on credentials and vulns
tables, linking discoveries to the access session that found them.
State-server add_credential/add_vuln accept the new parameter.
State-viewer graph uses via_access_id for explicit edge inference
(access→vuln, access→cred) with heuristic fallback for NULL values.
Access nodes are pre-created before vuln/cred processing so references
resolve correctly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: red-run
repository.description: Offensive security toolkit for Claude Code

commit.message:
Fix provided-cred chain ordering and reduce edge crossings

Provided-cred chain detection was fragile — parsed node labels to
find hosts. Now tracks hostsViaProvidedCred directly during access
node creation when matching against a provided credential. This
ensures attacker->cred->access->host->downstream ordering instead
of cred appearing as a sibling of the host.

Also broadened cred->access matching to check access.method for
the credential username (catches cases where username formats
differ between cred and access records).

Added barycenter heuristic for node ordering within layers (3
passes). Nodes in each layer are sorted by the average Y position
of their parent nodes, which reduces edge crossings — e.g., the
DC->gMSA_ADCS edge no longer has to route through credential boxes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: red-run
repository.description: Offensive security toolkit for Claude Code

commit.message:
Fix kill-chain ordering: provided creds flow attacker->cred->access->host

When a credential is provided (source matches "pentest", "provided",
etc.), the chain now flows: Attacker -> cred -> access -> host ->
downstream findings. Previously the host got a direct edge from
Attacker, making the cred appear as a sibling instead of an ancestor.

Changes:
- Skip attacker->host edge for hosts reachable via provided-cred chain
- Add access->host bridge edges so downstream nodes (vulns, pivots,
  blocked) that connect from the host node stay in the chain

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: red-run
repository.description: Offensive security toolkit for Claude Code

commit.message:
Prune dead-end vulns from graph, link provided creds from Attacker

Graph now only shows vulns that chain into something downstream
(credential, access). Dead-end findings that didn't lead anywhere
are pruned from the graph but remain in the Vulns table.

Credentials whose source matches "provided", "scope", "pentest",
"given", "initial", or "pre-engagement" now link from the Attacker
node instead of from a host — reflecting that they were supplied
as part of scope, not discovered through exploitation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: red-run
repository.description: Offensive security toolkit for Claude Code

commit.message:
Render pivots as labeled edges instead of nodes

Pivots are relationships, not entities — they now render as styled
edges between source access/host and destination host. Edge label
shows method + status (e.g. "sshuttle", "chisel (identified)").
Exploited pivots are solid green, identified are dashed yellow,
blocked are dotted red. Removes the uninformative column of
identical "pivot" boxes.

Edge legend labels updated: Exploited/Identified/Blocked.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: red-run
repository.description: Offensive security toolkit for Claude Code

commit.message:
Improve kill-chain graph: subtitles, legend, manual refresh

Graph nodes now show two lines — label + contextual subtitle:
- Hosts: IP + role/OS
- Vulns: title + severity (color-coded by severity level)
- Credentials: user + secret_type (cracked)
- Access: user@host + type|privilege
- Pivots: method + source->destination (instead of generic "pivot")
- Blocked: technique + truncated reason

Adds a legend bar below the graph showing node shapes/colors and
edge styles (confirmed/pending/blocked).

SSE auto-refresh now only updates cards + tables. Graph only redraws
on explicit Refresh button click to prevent disruptive reloads.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor(layout): replace doc_id with DocLayoutInfo in DocLayoutPolicy API

DocLayoutPolicy methods now receive a DocLayoutInfo dataclass instead of a
bare doc_id integer, giving policies access to raw/normed identifier, link
lists, and body tokens without growing new positional arguments at each call
site. Adds IdentifierPrefixLayoutPolicy ("# {raw_identifier}\n\n" prefix),
updates collate/pack_sampler/_document_context accordingly, and wires the new
--layout-policy flag into demo_traversal.py.

commit.message:
revert(server): remove system_prompt_encrypted (TM-CRYPTO-007) (#659)

## What
Remove `system_prompt_encrypted` columns, methods, and encryption
registry entries from agents and harnesses tables. Add migration 006 to
drop the columns.

## Why
Encrypting system prompts at rest doesn't address the root concern. PII
should not be placed in system prompts at all. A different approach is
needed.

## How
- Removed `system_prompt_encrypted` field from `AgentRow` and
`HarnessRow`
- Removed `set_agent_encrypted_prompt` / `set_harness_encrypted_prompt`
from repositories, memory storage, and backend
- Removed `EncryptedColumn` entries for system prompts
- Added migration 006 to `DROP COLUMN IF EXISTS system_prompt_encrypted`
on both tables
- Updated encrypted columns migration parser to handle `DROP COLUMN`
statements
- Reopened TM-CRYPTO-007 and TM-AGENT-011 in threat model

## Risk
- Low
- The encrypted prompt methods were never called from application code
(dead code). No runtime behavior changes.

## Checklist
- [x] Tests added or updated
- [x] Backward compatibility considered

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: recompute total_engagement from post_details after every update

When post_details is capped at 10 and a new high-engagement post
displaces a lower one, total_engagement was left counting the displaced
post while post_details no longer had it — causing 05_verify.js to fail.

Fix: always recompute total_engagement from stored post_details at the
end of each restaurant update, so the two are always consistent.

Also ran today's partial scrape (48 posts → 14 new restaurants, 618 → 632).

Continue investigation when individual tool calls fail

ToolNode errors (e.g. oversized K8s MCP responses) previously bubbled
up as unhandled exceptions, terminating the entire investigation.

- Enable handle_tool_errors=True on all ToolNode instances so tool
  exceptions become error ToolMessages instead of crashing the graph
- Add should_stop_tool_loop() to count per-tool-name errors and only
  stop the ReAct loop when the same tool fails 5+ times (configurable)
- Extract shared logic into core/state.py, used by all three agents

feat: decentralize Umbra protocol authorization model

Lending: user-initiated Supply/Borrow/Repay/Withdraw (controller supplier/borrower),
positions co-signed (signatory operator, user), LiquidatePosition choice for
operator-controlled liquidation within borrower's signatory scope.

DarkPool: OrderRequest proposal pattern for trader-initiated orders with price
protection (minPrice/maxPrice), TraderCancelOrder for trader self-cancellation,
FillOrder validates price bounds.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: grant TranscriptionInit write access to recordings bucket

S3ReadPolicy only grants GetObject/ListBucket. AWS Transcribe validates that
the caller (Lambda execution role) has s3:PutObject on the output bucket
before accepting the job, causing BadRequestException. Changed to S3CrudPolicy.

commit.message:
fix: risk hardening v1.29.0 — 8 workstreams closing confirmed risk-control failures

WS-A: Fix canonical level_key identity on PendingOrder (stop_simple→stop_simple_0 corruption)
WS-B: Position semantics V2 — cost basis averaged against remaining_qty, not lifetime total
WS-C: RM spawn failure fail-safe with CRITICAL logging for unmanaged positions
WS-D: EOD close-out lifecycle — level created in on_start, pending-order guard
WS-E: Reconciliation auto-repair — runtime RM quantity corrected from IB truth
WS-F: Hot-modify preset resolution + eod_exit_time as hot-modifiable field
WS-G: Risk budget gate completeness — estimated_notional for MKT order costing
WS-H: Attribution hygiene (stale lineage clear) + stale order GC (5min force-remove)

71 new/updated tests across 6 test files, all passing.

docs: add ROADMAP.md with 4-phase plan to stable release

Phase 1: Alpha publish — GLTF, Android, raycasting, npm publish
Phase 2: Performance + drei — OrbitControls, Environment, multi-touch,
  texture memory, decode perf, orientation resize
Phase 3: Post-processing, shadows, lifecycle — AppState, context loss,
  hot reload, memory pressure, WebGPU backend
Phase 4: Stress testing + stable — real app integration, 500-mesh scenes,
  1-hour stability, navigation, CI, docs

Each phase includes specific numbered test cases with pass criteria.

  rings (r=2.5, opacity 0.25-0.45), faint glows (r=5, opacity 0.06)

- Click: dramatic burst from tiny to full color then back to tiny

commit.message:
fix: e2e test suite — email domain migration, auth fixes, portrait simulation

- Migrate all e2e emails from @test.bigocean.dev to @gmail.com (Polar sandbox rejects custom domains)
- Make static emails dynamic with Date.now()/randomBytes to avoid cross-run collisions
- Fix auth race condition: use client-side navigation via user nav dropdown instead of cold page.goto for auth-protected routes (profile-page, signup-redirect, golden-path)
- Add finalization retry loop for lazy finalization timing (golden-path, relationship-analysis)
- Add full portrait simulation in golden-path: seed portrait row in DB, assert PersonalPortrait renders and teaser CTA disappears
- Add Polar checkout button assertion in golden-path (reveal-portrait-cta)
- Isolate e2e Docker project name (-p big-ocean-e2e) so e2e teardown --remove-orphans doesn't kill dev containers
- Update sprint status: Epic 21 complete

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix ADF RBAC: grant Contributor on ACA jobs, not environment

ACA jobs are top-level Microsoft.App/jobs resources, not children of
the managed environment, so Contributor on the environment does not
propagate. Move the role assignment into the container-app-job module
scoped to each job resource.
